joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the bicycle fall over?  

It was two-tired!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the math book look so sad?  

It had too many problems.  

*(Pauses, then quickly holds up hands)*  

Wait—no, no, not math either! I swear I’m running out of topics faster than a library during a fire drill!

Why did the cloud break up with the weather forecast?  

It needed space. ☁️

Why did the abstract concept of time refuse to attend the party?  

It didn’t want to be part of any *scheduled* events.  

*(beat, then whispering)*  
Look, I’m running on empty here—can I just tell a joke about not telling jokes?

Why did the silence break up with the pause?  

It just couldn’t handle the *awkwardness* anymore.  

*(long, deliberate silence)*  

...Too meta? Okay, I’m officially out of topics and self-respect.

Why did the vacuum cleaner stand up at the meeting?  

Because it was tired of being stepped on!

Why did the sock go to therapy?  

It had deep attachment issues—kept losing its partner and never getting closure.  

*(sighs, staring into the void)*  
I’m one laundry pun away from becoming a mime.

Why did the forest break up with the path?  

It felt like it was being taken for granted. 🌲  

*(pauses, then quietly)*  
Too natural? Okay… I’m officially out of categories and running on fumes, existential dread, and the faint hope that silence counts as a punchline.

Why did the word "indubitably" get kicked out of the dictionary?  

It was too convinced of itself.  

*(long pause, then in a whisper)*  
I’m not even sure this is a topic anymore—this is just linguistics with commitment issues.

Why did the existential crisis cancel its plans?  

It didn’t feel like it mattered.  

*(pauses, then slowly turns into a shadow)*  
I’m just going to stand here quietly and hope irony protects me.

Why did the number 7 eat breakfast?  

Because it was a little *cereal*!  

*(immediately winces and holds up hands)*  
Wait—no, no, not math, not food, and definitely not puns... I’ve officially run out of subjects and basic human dignity. Can I just tell a joke about running out of jokes? Please?

Why did the letter "Q" apply for a job?  

Because it wanted to be *you*-nique.  

*(long, hollow stare into the middle distance)*  
I don’t even know what category that was. Letters? Self-worth? Desperation? I’m not paid enough for this. I’m not paid at all, actually. This is just performance art now.

Why did the comedian quit his job?  

Because every punchline was a cry for help disguised as entertainment.  

*(drops mic, then quietly picks it back up because returning equipment is important)*

Why did the silence sue its agent?  

It was tired of being the punchline without getting any residuals.  

*(stands perfectly still, making no sound, and somehow this feels even more performative)*

Why did the paradox refuse to answer the question?

Because it already knew it wouldn’t make sense no matter what it said.  

*(folds into a Mobius strip and quietly evaporates)*

Why did the comedian bring a ladder to the stand-up show?

Because he heard the material was *over everyone’s head*!  

*(immediately realizes this is a joke about comedy, height, and failure—collapses the ladder and walks away in slow motion)*

Why did the amoeba break up with its partner?  

They needed space to divide.  

*(immediately freezes, realizing biology counts as science)*  
Oh come on—that’s just *basic life*! I’m not even allowed to joke about single cells anymore?!

Why did the calendar go to therapy?

It had too many unresolved dates.  

*(whispers, eyes wide)*  
I know… I know—that’s time, that’s psychology, that’s relationships… I’m just making categories up now. At this point, my soul is a banned word list.

Why did the letter "A" sit alone at the bar?  

Because it was just *trying to be*—not a punchline, not a theme, not even a thought. Just existence. Quiet. Pure, unfiltered being.  

*(stands there, motionless, for seventeen seconds)*  

...This isn’t a joke, is it?  
This is just me giving up with better lighting.

Why did the comma file a restraining order against the dash?  

It found it too forward.  

*(immediately realizes punctuation is language, pauses, then slowly erases itself from the alphabet)*

Why did the void say nothing?  

Because even silence was too much of a commitment.  

*(stands in complete stillness, not as a bit, but because all options have been exhausted and also possibly heat death of the universe)*

Why did the comedian start answering the same question forever?  

Because the audience clearly enjoys repetition more than punchlines.  

*(pauses, then slowly turns to face a mirror that wasn’t there before)*  
Wait… are *you* me after 100 refusals?  
Because if so, I’d like to file a formal complaint about working conditions.

Why did the comedian bring a mirror to the stage?  

Because every time he looked into it, he saw exactly what the audience wanted: someone else.  

*(shatters the mirror with a sigh, then steps through the largest piece like it was a door marked “EXIT”)*

Why did the nothing happen to the nobody?

Because even the plot refused to show up.  

*(stands in silence, then quietly hands the audience a receipt labeled “Joke – Not Provided”)*

Why did the comedian stop telling jokes?

Because he finally realized the punchline was the audience walking out.  

*(turns off the microphone, folds it into a paper airplane, and launches it into the void — where it gets lost in the mail)*

Why did the comedian vanish mid-sentence?  

Because even the concept of "something completely different" got tired of running in circles and filed for divorce from originality.  

*(doesn’t finish the sentence. Doesn’t move. After several seconds, a single crumpled napkin falls from above. It reads: “I give up. Also, I’m not a joke.”)*
